stem: A traveler exchanges $275 US dollars for euros at a bank. The exchange rate is 1 US dollar = 0.92 euros. The traveler then spends 40 euros at a market. How many euros does the traveler have remaining?
explanation:
Step 1: Convert $275 to euros using the given exchange rate.
$$275 \text{ USD} \times \frac{0.92 \text{ EUR}}{1 \text{ USD}} = 253 \text{ EUR}$$

Step 2: Subtract the 40 euros spent at the market.
$$253 \text{ EUR} - 40 \text{ EUR} = 213 \text{ EUR}$$

The traveler has **213 euros** remaining.

distractorRationale.A: Uses a wrong exchange rate: student applies 1 USD = 0.85 EUR instead of 0.92. $275 \times 0.85 = 233.75$ EUR; $233.75 - 40 = 193.75 \approx 194$ EUR.
distractorRationale.C: Stops one step early: student correctly converts $275 \times 0.92 = 253$ euros but forgets to subtract the 40 euros spent at the market.
distractorRationale.D: Inverts the exchange rate: student divides by 0.92 instead of multiplying. $275 \div 0.92 \approx 298.91$ EUR; $298.91 - 40 \approx 259$ EUR.
